channel.title: JLT Daily Dispatch
channel.description: The JLT Daily Dispatch with Sophie Skies — daily travel news for agents

item.title: 🌍 The Canaries have been clever about this one.
item.description:
🌍 The Canaries have been clever about this one.
Instead of following the mandatory-levy route that other European destinations have gone down, they have launched REGNEXT: a voluntary tourism fund that lets visitors choose to contribute to habitat restoration, biodiversity projects and affordable housing across all seven islands.
18.39 million tourists visited in 2025. That is a record. And the government's response has been to build something that invites people in rather than charging them at the door.
Jet2, TUI and easyJet Holidays are already signed-up partners, which means this has genuine reach.
✨ Why does this matter for you? Because clients are asking more sustainability questions before they book. REGNEXT is a simple, clear answer. And having that answer is the difference between an agent who has been paying attention and one who has not.
Full Dispatch in the comments 📣
~ The JLT Daily Despatch with 📣 Sophie Skies — Your Daily Travel News Scout
First comment: https://janinelovestravel.co.uk/canary-islands-regnext-voluntary-tourism-fund

item.title: 🌍 Virgin Voyages has turned July into a proper agent appreciation month.
item.description:
🌍 Virgin Voyages has turned July into a proper agent appreciation month.
Triple rewards on bookings. A "Bring on Mexico" fam trip giveaway. An entire month dedicated to the trade rather than a quick flash window.
Rather than a tight deadline to chase, they have committed the whole of July to saying thank you to agents who have been selling the line. That is a different kind of gesture.
🧳 If you are actively selling Virgin Voyages, or have been curious about it, this is a good moment to pay attention. Triple rewards accumulate across bookings that come in naturally during July, and the Mexico fam is a genuine opportunity to win a trip that changes how confidently you talk about their itineraries to clients.
Full Dispatch in the comments 📣
~ The JLT Daily Despatch with 📣 Sophie Skies — Your Daily Travel News Scout
First comment: https://janinelovestravel.co.uk/virgin-voyages-triple-rewards-mexico-fam-july-2026

item.title: Fancy a fam trip that pays back? ⛵
item.description:
Fancy a fam trip that pays back? ⛵
Lisa Holdroff at Newell's Travel in Falmouth joined a 4-night Star Clippers sailing from Malaga to Nice. Two weeks after she got home, she'd booked 7 passengers across several Star Clippers sailings… ✨
Total value? £33,000.
What she did next is the bit worth borrowing. She wrote an article for Falmouth Life magazine, built a window display, blogged about it on the agency website, and picked up the phone to clients who fit the profile. Four channels, one fam trip, one very good fortnight.
Her line on it: "clients recognise genuine enthusiasm... this isn't cruising in the traditional sense." 🌍
And the timing's handy — Star Clippers is currently running reduced-rate agent sailings in July and August.
Full blog on the website 👇 link in the first comment
~ The JLT Daily Despatch with 📣 Sophie Skies — Your Daily Travel News Scout
https://janinelovestravel.co.uk/star-clippers-fam-trip-agent-booking

item.title: Mauritius is the easy nudge to make this week 🌍
item.description:
Mauritius is the easy nudge to make this week 🌍
159,164 UK seats sit on sale every year on the London Gatwick to SSR non-stop, across five flights a week, and the May to October shoulder season is where the softer pricing lives without writing the weather off. If you've got clients who've half-mentioned Mauritius and never quite booked it, that's the lever to pull.
The story around the destination is 'slow luxury' ✨ wellness, 15 golf courses, kitesurfing, diving, and a growing MICE offering. A nicer way back into Mauritius for clients who think they already know it.
Behind all of that, Air Mauritius and the MTPA ran a UK roadshow in June, and Chief Commercial Officer Laurent Recoura has said openly that he wants to grow to daily non-stop at the earliest opportunity. No firm date, but three more A350-900s are on order, first one due December 2026, so the fleet is being built to match.
Air Mauritius will be at WTM London this November if you'd like to put a face to the airline.
Full blog on the website 👇 link in the first comment
~ The JLT Daily Despatch with 📣 Sophie Skies — Your Daily Travel News Scout
First comment: https://janinelovestravel.co.uk/air-mauritius-non-stop-london-gatwick

item.title: Travel Weekly is calling it a "bloodbath"… a potential price drop predicted in late summer, splashed across the trade press this morning. ☀️
item.description:
Travel Weekly is calling it a "bloodbath"… a potential price drop predicted in late summer, splashed across the trade press this morning. ☀️
Which means by Friday, at least one of your clients will have seen the headline and rung you wondering if they should hold off. Here's the line you can use… "Travel Weekly is flagging a potential price drop in late summer. If you're flexible on dates, we can hold an option and watch the market together for a week. If you're not flexible, locking in now is the safer play because the good rooms in the good resorts thin out fast once the bargain-hunters move." 💭
Honest, useful, no hard sell. You've given them a reason to trust you and a reason to keep the call going.
Late bookings into July are already building, and the UK heatwave is splitting trade opinion on top of it, so the late window is busy this week. The clients who book are the ones whose agent sounded confident on the phone. ✈️
Full blog on the website 👇 link in the first comment
~ The JLT Daily Despatch with 📣 Sophie Skies — Your Daily Travel News Scout
https://janinelovestravel.co.uk/late-sales-build-uk-heatwave

item.title: 🌊 Globetrender's just dropped its 2027 Med outlook, and there's a lot in here you can use with your luxury clients this week.
item.description:
🌊 Globetrender's just dropped its 2027 Med outlook, and there's a lot in here you can use with your luxury clients this week.
The Mediterranean welcomed more than 300 million tourists in 2025, and 90% of European travellers are staying in Europe again this summer. The interesting bit? They don't want the same Med holiday twice.
Three trends to keep in your back pocket:
✨ Micro Cultures — Albania, Delos, the smaller Greek Islands. Honey-harvesting with beekeepers, ancient ruins off the main trail. Your clients want past the headline sights.
💡 Blue Mind Experiences — water proximity isn't incidental anymore, it's the design of the stay. MGallery and NIKO Seaside Resort in Crete are already building around it.
🧭 The Comeback Effect — repeat visitors want depth on the second trip. The unspoilt beach, the bistro, the winemaker. As Jenny Southan at Globetrender puts it, clients want "a meaningful relationship with the Med."
Useful for any returning-client chat this week 💭
Full blog on the website 👇 link in the first comment
~ The JLT Daily Despatch with 📣 Sophie Skies, Your Daily Travel News Scout
First comment: https://janinelovestravel.co.uk/mediterranean-luxury-travel-trends-2027

item.title: 🌍 Riviera Travel has dropped its mid-year update, and the trade is right at the heart of the story.
item.description:
🌍 Riviera Travel has dropped its mid-year update, and the trade is right at the heart of the story.
Passengers booked through UK agents are up 27% year-on-year, and revenue through agents is up 33%. River cruise is leading the charge with passenger numbers up 33% and revenue up 35%. Touring is moving nicely too at +10% passengers and +19% revenue.
✨ The solo ship George Eliot is already nearly 40% sold for its first season, and the CEO has hinted that a second solo vessel could be on the cards. Worth knowing if you've got solo travellers on your books waiting for something special.
💡 Two new agent tools are now live: Riviera Explorer (the trade platform) and Riva AI (an AI assistant for sales and client engagement). Both built with your day in mind.
If you've been recommending Riviera this year, these numbers have your name on them.
Full blog on the website 👇 link in the first comment
~ The JLT Daily Despatch with 📣 Sophie Skies, Your Daily Travel News Scout
First comment: https://janinelovestravel.co.uk/riviera-travel-agent-success-2026

item.title: 🚢 Big one for cruise sellers this week… Cunard has run its first-ever dedicated homeworker fam trip aboard Queen Mary 2, and 21 agents from across the trade (including the JLT community) got to walk the ship properly.
item.description:
🚢 Big one for cruise sellers this week… Cunard has run its first-ever dedicated homeworker fam trip aboard Queen Mary 2, and 21 agents from across the trade (including the JLT community) got to walk the ship properly.
The route was Southampton to Le Havre to Cherbourg on 5 June, and the agents on board got hands-on with cabins, suites, Britannia Club, restaurants and entertainment. The kind of ship-knowledge that completely changes the way you sell a QM2 crossing to a client.
✨ Here's where it gets interesting for you… Cunard's UK team called the fam "long overdue" and committed to running more. Homeworker bookings have already brought in £200,000+ across the channel, so this isn't a one-off. If Cunard is on your radar, keep an eye out for the next call.
QM2 is still the world's only ocean liner, which is a lovely line to drop into client chats this week. 🧭
Full blog on the website 👇 link in the first comment
~ The JLT Daily Despatch with 📣 Sophie Skies — Your Daily Travel News Scout
First comment: https://janinelovestravel.co.uk/cunard-queen-mary-2-homeworker-fam-trip

item.title: ✈️ Gulf travel is officially back on the table for your UK clients… and the timing is genuinely in your favour.
item.description:
✈️ Gulf travel is officially back on the table for your UK clients… and the timing is genuinely in your favour.
The FCDO lifted its advisory on the UAE, Qatar and Saudi Arabia on 18 June, with Australia moving the same day. Dubai, Abu Dhabi, Doha, Riyadh, Jeddah… all back on the official "go" list.
Here's the bit that's interesting for you 💡. EASA hasn't cleared Gulf airspace for European carriers yet, with Lufthansa, Air France and KLM still grounded on those routes until around October. UK carriers ARE flying. So if a client is comparing you to a European online competitor right now, that competitor probably can't get them to Dubai on a direct flag-carrier route. You can.
A quick check before you confirm: which airline is on the booking 🌍, what does the FCDO page say today, and does the operator have its own risk line running alongside.
I know this is your week, not merely an industry story… but if you've had Gulf enquiries on pause, this is the nudge to pick them back up.
Full blog on the website 👇 link in the first comment
~ The JLT Daily Despatch with 📣 Sophie Skies — Your Daily Travel News Scout
First comment: https://janinelovestravel.co.uk/gulf-travel-advisory-lifted-uk-agents

item.title: ✈️ Tunisia is back on Jet2's map from May 2027… and it's a properly useful one to know about.
item.description:
✈️ Tunisia is back on Jet2's map from May 2027… and it's a properly useful one to know about.
Jet2 confirmed last week that flights and holidays to Tunisia restart from 1 May 2027, with eight weekly departures across Birmingham, East Midlands, Leeds Bradford, Manchester and Stansted. 🌍 Enfidha airport is the main hub, which puts your clients straight into Hammamet's beach zone.
Tunisia has been off Jet2's schedule since 2015, so this is a genuinely fresh destination for a lot of agents to get to grips with. Good news? It's package-led from day one, so it's a full bookable product, not flight-only. ⭐
For your budget-conscious beach clients who've done the Spanish islands a few times too many, this is one to start gently mentioning in conversations now. Hammamet hotels, Roman ruins, Djerba a hop south… all the makings of a holiday that feels a little different without going long-haul. 📍
A quiet first-mover opportunity for the agents who do their homework over winter.
Full blog on the website 👇 link in the first comment
~ The JLT Daily Despatch with 📣 Sophie Skies — Your Daily Travel News Scout
First comment: https://janinelovestravel.co.uk/jet2-tunisia-return-2027-agents

item.title: Qantas has just confirmed Sydney to London as the first Project Sunrise route, with the non-stop service launching October 2027.
item.description:
Qantas has just confirmed Sydney to London as the first Project Sunrise route, with the non-stop service launching October 2027.
It ends almost 80 years of stopovers on the Kangaroo Route. Back in 1947, the same journey took four days and seven stops… now it’s 22 hours, one flight, no transit.
The aircraft is a custom Airbus A350-1000ULR with an extra 20,000-litre fuel tank, and Qantas has ordered 12 of them. Tickets are expected on sale February 2027, so there’s plenty of runway to build the knowledge before your clients start asking.
Worth holding onto: the existing Perth-London and Sydney-Singapore-London services aren’t going anywhere, and Sydney-New York is confirmed as the next Sunrise route. So if you’ve got a client weighing up Australia for 2027 or 2028, you’ve now got three different shapes of the same trip to talk through.
Full blog on the website 👇 link in the first comment
~ The JLT Daily Despatch with 📣 Sophie Skies, Your Daily Travel News Scout
**First comment:** https://janinelovestravel.co.uk/qantas-confirms-sydney-london-non-stop-from-october-2027

item.title: Heads up on a useful EES update for any of you with clients heading to the Schengen Area this summer.
item.description:
Heads up on a useful EES update for any of you with clients heading to the Schengen Area this summer.
As The Independent reports, EU airports can temporarily pause the new biometric registration step if queues become unmanageable. Frontex confirmed it at an ABTA conference this week, calling it a “last resort” rather than a routine option.
Good to know: the flexibility is built into the regulation, the call sits with individual member states, and it ends in September. UK travellers have been warned of waits up to 6 hours at peak times, with delays already reported in Portugal, Spain and Italy.
The friendly framing for your clients? Arrive a little earlier than usual, expect the first Schengen trip to take longer at the border, and know that repeat trips move faster once biometric enrolment is done.
Full blog on the website 👇 link in the first comment
~ The JLT Daily Despatch with 📣 Sophie Skies, Your Daily Travel News Scout
**First comment:** https://janinelovestravel.co.uk/eu-border-queues-ees-suspension-summer

item.title: Right, this one is going to feel familiar the moment you read it.
item.description:
Right, this one is going to feel familiar the moment you read it.
Travel Weekly's exclusive tracking data is showing consumer confidence has rallied significantly since late April, and the proportion of people planning a holiday in the next three months has risen with it. Good news? Absolutely. The wrinkle is that the booking window has shrunk at the same time. Your clients are firmly in the "we're going" camp without quite being in the "we've booked" camp.
If your inbox has felt strangely quiet and then strangely loud in bursts, you are not imagining it, and you are not the only one. This is the late-summer squeeze in real time.
The opportunity sits in the gap. Confidence is the hard bit and that part is already done. The nudge between intention and confirmation is where you come in… a gentle re-warm of an April quote, a quick check-in with the “we should book something” crowd, a looser evening inbox for the next month.
Full blog on the website 👇 link in the first comment
~ The JLT Daily Despatch with Sophie Skies, Your Daily Travel News Scout
First comment: https://janinelovestravel.co.uk/consumer-confidence-booking-windows-june-2026

item.title: The Tartan Army are heading to the 2026 World Cup, and the ESTA gremlins are already at work. 🌍
item.description:
The Tartan Army are heading to the 2026 World Cup, and the ESTA gremlins are already at work. 🌍
Travel Weekly reported this week that Scotland fans are running into ESTA problems before they fly, with some applications delayed and others refused outright. Scotland's First Minister has stepped in to help, and a Scottish travel agency has been singled out for getting one client safely through the paperwork.
Here is the bit your clients will not know… once an ESTA is refused, the Visa Waiver door closes for that trip. The traveller then has to apply for a full US visa, which means an embassy appointment and a wait measured in weeks, not minutes.
You spot that risk early. A solo booker often does not find out until the check-in desk. ✨
A lovely real-world reminder that the quiet work you do behind the scenes is the work that saves the holiday.
Full blog on the website 👇 link in the first comment
~ The JLT Daily Despatch with Sophie Skies, Your Daily Travel News Scout
First comment: https://janinelovestravel.co.uk/esta-world-cup-agents-june-2026

item.title: A genuinely lovely week to be writing this one for you.
item.description:
A genuinely lovely week to be writing this one for you.
Travel Weekly reported on Friday that UK agencies have had one of their strongest weeks of trading this year, with Barrhead Travel calling it their best year-on-year week since March. Across the wider sector the picture’s the same… late demand is intensifying, summer last-minutes are flying, and the June surge has properly arrived.
If your inbox has felt busier this week, your gut is reading it right. The clients calling now tend to be ready to book. They’ve done the dithering, they’ve checked the dates, and they want a holiday in the diary.
Worth a quiet nod to yourself for the groundwork that’s turned into this. The April newsletters, the May supplier calls, the spring check-ins with quiet clients… that’s the work that turns into a strong June week. Take the booking, enjoy the bounce, and have a proper coffee on it.
Full blog on the website 👇 link in the first comment
~ The JLT Daily Despatch with Sophie Skies, Your Daily Travel News Scout
First comment: https://janinelovestravel.co.uk/travel-agencies-strong-trading-june-2026

item.title: 🌍 A brand-new long-haul carrier has officially touched down at Heathrow, and your clients will be asking about it.
item.description:
🌍 A brand-new long-haul carrier has officially touched down at Heathrow, and your clients will be asking about it.
Riyadh Air’s first ever commercial flight (RX401) landed at London Heathrow at 7.30am BST yesterday, after departing Riyadh at 2.35am local time. The aircraft is the Boeing 787-9 Dreamliner, with four cabin classes from Business Elite down to Economy. ✈️
London–Riyadh is operating daily. Manchester–Riyadh follows on 23 July 2026, three times a week. Tickets for both are on sale now.
Worth knowing the basics… the loyalty programme is called Sfeer, the onboard products come from Saudi lifestyle brand Kayanee, and the network already connects through Jeddah, Cairo, Dubai and Madrid. Backed by Saudi Arabia’s Public Investment Fund, with plans for 100+ destinations by 2030. 🇸🇦
If your inbox lights up with weekend “have you seen this?” messages, now you’re ready.
Full blog on the website 👇 link in the first comment
~ The JLT Daily Despatch with Sophie Skies, Your Daily Travel News Scout

item.title: 🌍 Good news from the Caribbean this morning.
item.description:
🌍 Good news from the Caribbean this morning.
The St Kitts Tourism Authority has renewed its UK agent discount programme, with discounted hotel rates at ten participating properties on the island, plus on-island benefits for agents who get out to see it for themselves. 🧳
It’s a renewal, not a launch, which means the scheme already has a run behind it and the tourist board has decided to keep it going. ✨
For agents with Caribbean clients on the desk, it’s a tidy one to file away. St Kitts tends to sit slightly under the radar next to the bigger Caribbean names, which is often where the more knowing recommendations come from. Worth a quick email to the UK office for current details on which hotels are in and how to apply.
Full blog on the website 👇 link in the first comment
~ The JLT Daily Despatch with Sophie Skies, Your Daily Travel News Scout

item.title: Big consumer story to be aware of this morning, agents…
item.description:
Big consumer story to be aware of this morning, agents…
Citizens Advice says more than 4 million people in Britain ran into problems with a package holiday in the past year. 34% of package bookers reported an issue, and the charity is now taking around 14 complaints a day. The biggest cluster? All-inclusive trips booked abroad, where quality issues and customer service failures stack up fastest. 1 in 4 affected travellers said it left them stressed or upset.
Here’s where it gets interesting for us. This is the kind of headline that gets noticed over breakfast… and gently reminds your clients why having a real person in their corner matters. You don’t need to sell it. You only need to be the one they ring first when something feels off.
A simple “if anything’s not right, call me before you call anyone else” goes a long way this week.
Full blog on the website 👇 link in the first comment
~ The JLT Daily Despatch with 📣 Sophie Skies — Your Daily Travel News Scout
First comment: https://janinelovestravel.co.uk/package-holiday-problems-why-an-agent-matters

item.title: Oceania Cruises has been running a competition with the UK trade to test product knowledge… and the results were lovely.
item.description:
Oceania Cruises has been running a competition with the UK trade to test product knowledge… and the results were lovely. The line walked away genuinely impressed by the depth of know-how across the room, with Clare Sacco taking the headline win.
Even better? Oceania’s sales team has followed the competition up with a pledge to widen its fam trip programme so more agents can get on board the ships and see the product first-hand.
For agents selling in the premium-cruise space, that’s a quiet vote of confidence in what you already do… and a heads-up to keep an eye on fam trip openings when they drop. The clients who book Oceania want a cruise that feels considered, and that’s a story you tell better after you’ve sailed the ship.
Full blog on the website 👇 link in the first comment
~ The JLT Daily Despatch with 📣 Sophie Skies, Your Daily Travel News Scout
First comment: https://janinelovestravel.co.uk/oceania-cruises-competition-strength-of-knowledge

item.title: 🌍 Sophie’s been keeping half an eye on Southampton this week, and there’s a line from Clia UK 2026 worth sharing with the group…
item.description:
🌍 Sophie’s been keeping half an eye on Southampton this week, and there’s a line from Clia UK 2026 worth sharing with the group…
More than 800 delegates filled the conference, and cruise line leadership openly flagged a “great opportunity” for UK agents to help fill late summer capacity. That is a public nudge from the people running the ships, not a marketing email.
💡 The backdrop is global. The Middle East conflict is still affecting cruise markets internationally, and traditional booking flows from some regions have softened. The UK trade has held up well, which is exactly why the chiefs are looking our way for the late-availability cabins.
So… maybe a quick scan through your active enquiries this week? Couples with flexible diaries, families eyeing a half-term tweak, the regular cruisers who routinely check your inbox for “anything worth a look.” A warm nudge with two or three suitable options often beats a glossy supplier email.
Full blog on the website 👇 link in the first comment
~ The JLT Daily Despatch with 📣 Sophie Skies, Your Daily Travel News Scout
First comment: https://janinelovestravel.co.uk/cruise-late-capacity-opportunity-uk-agents

item.title: ✨ Quick one worth your attention this week, agents.
item.description:
✨ Quick one worth your attention this week, agents.
Silversea has launched a UK trade referral initiative, and it’s one of those refreshingly clean ones: introduce a brand-new client to Silversea, they book a cruise, you earn £100. No tiers, no complicated process.
🛳 The window is short — it runs from 1 June to 31 July 2026 — and it’s specifically built to reward you for bringing first-time Silversea guests into the fold.
Worth a scan of your client book today. Past luxury cruisers, premium tour clients, anyone who has been circling something different for late 2026 or 2027… those are your referrals.
🌍 And remember: Silversea sits at the ultra-luxury end of the cruise market and has been part of the Royal Caribbean Group since 2018, so you’re introducing your clients to a serious name.
Two months to make this work. Pick up the phone.
Full blog on the website 👇 link in the first comment
~ The JLT Daily Despatch with Sophie Skies, Your Daily Travel News Scout
First comment: https://janinelovestravel.co.uk/silversea-100-referral-initiative-uk-agents

item.title: Qantas has flown the plane that could finally make Sydney non-stop a real conversation with your clients. ✈️
item.description:
Qantas has flown the plane that could finally make Sydney non-stop a real conversation with your clients. ✈️
On 2 June, the Project Sunrise Airbus A350-1000ULR completed its maiden test flight from Toulouse, a 3 hour 43 minute loop over France with Airbus’ test crew on board. It’s the aircraft built specifically for non-stop routes of up to 22 hours, thanks to an extra 20,000-litre rear centre fuel tank.
Now around 80 hours of flight testing kicks off across the next two months. ✨
Here’s the bit worth holding onto. Qantas has confirmed it will announce the first Project Sunrise route and the timing of inaugural commercial services later this month. Sydney to London and Sydney to New York are both in the mix, non-stop, for the first time in commercial aviation history. 🌍
If you have clients with Australia on their list, this is your cue to flag it on the next call. Be the agent who tells them first.
Full blog on the website 👇 link in the first comment
~ The JLT Daily Despatch with Sophie Skies, Your Daily Travel News Scout
First comment: https://janinelovestravel.co.uk/qantas-project-sunrise-first-flight-london-sydney
